curiosities.name: A Small Brass Compass
curiosities.description: It does not point north. It points consistently in a specific direction that is not north and adjusts as they travel to maintain a consistent bearing toward something that moves with them or is very far away.
curiosities.dmNote: DM decides what the compass is pointing at. Could be the Letavec den. Could be something else entirely. Leave it mysterious.

curiosities.name: A Small Carved Figure
curiosities.description: Stone, well-worn, carved into a shape that is almost human but not quite. The proportions are wrong in a specific way that is hard to articulate. Spurt immediately wants it and cannot explain why.
curiosities.spurtReaction: He picks it up before anyone else can reach it. At Dread 41 or higher he talks to it quietly when he thinks nobody is listening.

curiosities.name: A Silver Locket
curiosities.description: Contains a small painted portrait of a person. Old. The person looks disturbingly like a party member — not exactly, but enough. Engraved on the back with initials and a date from forty years ago.
curiosities.narrativeHook: AI assigns the resemblance to whichever party member the connection would be most dramatically interesting for.

curiosities.name: A Key
curiosities.description: Iron, heavy, old. Opens something. The lock it opens is not here. Worn smooth where a hand held it for years. Whatever it opens was important enough to carry this key a long time.
curiosities.dmNote: The lock exists somewhere in the campaign. DM places it. The key finding the lock is a session moment.

curiosities.name: A Sealed Letter
curiosities.description: Wax seal intact. Addressed in a language the party may or may not read. Breaking the seal reads the letter. Not breaking it preserves whatever relationship the seal represents.
curiosities.dmNote: The choice of whether to break the seal is itself the treasure.
